title: Doctor lint core: checks #16-18 (orphan demote, stale archive, pending overdue) — read-side, no mutation
scope: packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts (extend additive checks list pattern at L285-305 with 3 new createCheck functions; lint mode gating)
description: Add 3 new doctor lint checks following the additive-checks-array pattern established at packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts:285-305 (rc.3 added check #15 at commit 0bc92a1; rc.4 grows total to 18 after this task). All 3 checks are READ-SIDE in this task: they compute findings but do NOT mutate files or emit events. The mutation + event-emission half lands in TASK-003 (--apply-lint). Lint-mode gating is via a `mode: 'lint' | 'fix' | 'check'` parameter the new checks read from a shared context object — when mode !== 'lint', the new checks short-circuit and contribute zero findings (preserves existing doctor invocations).

files.target: checks[] array at ~L285-305 + 3 new createCheck functions
files.change: Add 3 new lint-mode check factories: `createOrphanDemoteCheck` (scans canonical .fabric/knowledge/{team,personal}/<type>/*.md; computes inactivity per-file via events.jsonl scan for last knowledge_proposed/promoted/plan_context_fetch event with matching stable_id; threshold = 90d for maturity=stable, 30d for endorsed, 14d for draft; finding kind='lint:orphan_demote' with proposed_action='demote_one_tier' + before/after maturity), `createStaleArchiveCheck` (scans canonical entries with maturity=draft AND no activity for 90d MORE — i.e., already-demoted entries that have not been touched again; finding kind='lint:stale_archive' with proposed_action='archive_to_dot_archive' + target_path .fabric/.archive/<type>/<filename>), `createPendingOverdueCheck` (scans .fabric/knowledge/pending/<type>/*.md; flag entries where now - file mtime OR frontmatter.created_at > 14d; kind='lint:pending_overdue', NO mutation proposed — report only). All three short-circuit when ctx.mode !== 'lint' (preserves existing doctor caller behavior). Push the 3 factories into checks[] at the rc.3 insertion point.

Add a new describe block 'lint mode checks (rc.4 TASK-001)' with at least 9 unit tests: orphan_demote_no_findings_when_mode_is_not_lint, orphan_demote_finds_stable_entry_with_90d_inactivity, orphan_demote_finds_endorsed_entry_with_30d_inactivity, orphan_demote_finds_draft_entry_with_14d_inactivity, orphan_demote_skips_recently_active_entry, stale_archive_finds_draft_with_additional_90d_quiet, stale_archive_skips_active_demoted_entry, pending_overdue_flags_entry_older_than_14d, pending_overdue_skips_recent_pending. Each test seeds a tmpdir .fabric/ tree, optionally appends events.jsonl entries to set last-active-time, invokes runDoctor(projectRoot, {mode:'lint'}), asserts the relevant finding kind appears (or does not appear) with expected target paths.

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L1-50 (imports + types) and L285-305 (checks array assembly) to lock the createCheck factory shape and the runDoctor signature; identify how rc.3 createKnowledgePromotedSynthesizedCheck wires in (commit 0bc92a1) — mirror that exact shape

Define 3 new createCheck factory functions at the bottom of doctor.ts (or in a new helper section before the checks[] array): createOrphanDemoteCheck(ctx), createStaleArchiveCheck(ctx), createPendingOverdueCheck(ctx); each returns an object {id, run: async () => Finding[]} matching the existing check shape

createOrphanDemoteCheck.run: walk projectRoot/.fabric/knowledge/<layer>/<type>/*.md for layer in [team, personal] and type in [decisions, pitfalls, guidelines, models, processes]; for each file, parse frontmatter to extract stable_id + maturity; read events.jsonl tail to find last event matching stable_id (knowledge_proposed | knowledge_promoted | plan_context with matching id); compute now - lastActiveAt; threshold map: stable=90d, endorsed=30d, draft=14d; if exceeded, emit Finding{kind:'lint:orphan_demote', target_path:relativePath, severity:'info', message, proposed_action:{type:'demote', from:maturity, to:next_lower(maturity)}}

createStaleArchiveCheck.run: same file walk; for each file with maturity=draft (post-demote candidates), check that last activity was >180d ago (90d demote threshold + 90d additional quiet); if exceeded, emit Finding{kind:'lint:stale_archive', target_path:relativePath, proposed_action:{type:'archive', dest:'.fabric/.archive/<type>/<filename>'}}; this check assumes orphan_demote already ran (transitively, since draft + 90d additional == 180d total inactivity for previously stable entry, or 120d for previously endorsed, or 90d for born-draft)

createPendingOverdueCheck.run: walk projectRoot/.fabric/knowledge/pending/<type>/*.md; for each file read frontmatter.created_at OR stat.mtime; if now - createdAt > 14d, emit Finding{kind:'lint:pending_overdue', target_path:relativePath, severity:'warn', message, proposed_action:null} — REPORT ONLY
All 3 factories must short-circuit at the top of run(): `if (ctx.mode !== 'lint') return [];` — this preserves existing doctor (no flag) + doctor --fix invocations (mode='fix' or 'check')
Wire context: extend the runDoctor signature (or wrap in a thin caller helper) to accept mode parameter; default 'check'; pass through to ctx; existing callers unaffected

Insert the 3 new factories into the checks[] array at the rc.3 insertion point (after createKnowledgePromotedSynthesizedCheck); preserve alphabetical ordering of createCheck factory names if convention exists, else insert at end of new-rc.4 group

Add doctor.test.ts new describe block 'lint mode checks (rc.4 TASK-001)' with the 9 unit tests; mirror existing test seed pattern (tmpdir, write fixture .md files, append events.jsonl, invoke runDoctor with mode:'lint', assert findings)

Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server typecheck && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test`; verify 0 type errors and all 9 new tests pass; commit as 'feat(doctor): lint checks #16-18 orphan-demote/stale-archive/pending-overdue (rc.4 TASK-001)'

reference.pattern: Additive-checks-array pattern in doctor.ts; rc.3 added check #15 (createKnowledgePromotedSynthesizedCheck) at commit 0bc92a1 — mirror that exact shape; lint-mode gating via ctx.mode short-circuit
reference.examples: rc.3 commit 0bc92a1 (filesystem-edit fallback as check #15); rc.2 commit 8dfa018 (createKnowledgeProposedCheck); event-ledger reading via existing readEventLedger helper

criteria: doctor.ts has 18 checks total (15 pre-rc.4 + 3 new); checks[] array length === 18 verified via test that imports CHECKS const or counts via runDoctor with all-finding-types fixture

createOrphanDemoteCheck emits Finding when stable entry inactive for 90d (asserted by seeding events.jsonl with last event 91d old + maturity:stable; runDoctor mode:'lint'; expect 1 lint:orphan_demote finding with target_path matching seeded file)
createOrphanDemoteCheck respects per-maturity thresholds: stable=90d, endorsed=30d, draft=14d (3 separate test cases verify each threshold boundary at +1d past)
createStaleArchiveCheck emits Finding only when entry has maturity=draft AND additional 90d quiet beyond initial demote threshold (asserted by 91d-quiet draft fixture vs 89d-quiet draft fixture)
createPendingOverdueCheck emits Finding when pending entry > 14d old based on frontmatter.created_at OR mtime fallback (asserted by 15d-old pending fixture vs 13d-old pending fixture)
All 3 new checks short-circuit (return []) when ctx.mode !== 'lint' — verified by runDoctor without mode flag (default 'check') asserting 0 lint:* findings
0 file mutations performed by any of the 3 checks (doctor --lint is read-only; verified by pre/post snapshot of .fabric/ tree contents)
0 events emitted by any of the 3 checks (events.jsonl byte-count stable across runDoctor invocation; verified by readFileSync size before/after)
doctor.test.ts new describe block has >=9 tests; all pass; pre-existing doctor tests remain green (no regression)
New code in doctor.ts (rc.4 added region) achieves >=90% lines coverage in vitest run --coverage (manual check; rc4-coverage-gate.mjs in TASK-010)

rationale.chosen_approach: Extend existing additive-checks-array pattern with 3 lint-mode-gated factories; ctx.mode short-circuit preserves backward compat; read-only in this task, mutation in TASK-003
alternatives_considered: New file packages/server/src/services/doctor-lint.ts as separate module — rejected: existing checks[] aggregation is in doctor.ts; cross-file imports would fragment the additive pattern
Extend Finding union type with new lint:* kind discriminator — accepted (this approach uses kind as marker, no schema change since Finding.kind is open string)
Land all 6 checks in one task — rejected: doctor.ts already 1593 LOC; 6-check PR unreviewable
Default mode to 'lint' (always-on) — rejected: changes existing doctor behavior; would surface findings on every existing invocation; opt-in is safer
decision_factors: Backward compat: existing doctor / doctor --fix callers must see zero behavioral change
Reviewability: 3-check PRs each ~150-300 LOC vs 1 PR with 800+ LOC
Read-side first: separating findings from mutations reduces test complexity (no event-ledger mocking needed in TASK-001)
Pattern reuse: rc.3 0bc92a1 establishes the exact factory shape to mirror
rationale.tradeoffs: Splitting lint authoring across TASK-001+002+003 means doctor.ts gets 3 commits in series; mitigated by atomic-commit-per-task convention preserving git bisect; final file ~2200 LOC after rc.4 (acceptable for a service module)

manual_checks: After TASK-001, run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server build` — verify no type errors

Manually invoke runDoctor(projectRoot, {mode:'lint'}) on Fabric self-repo — confirm findings array contains lint:orphan_demote / lint:stale_archive / lint:pending_overdue items if any apply (likely 0 in fresh repo; verifies mode plumbs through)
Verify existing `fabric doctor` and `fabric doctor --fix` CLI surfaces still work without regression
success_metrics: doctor.test.ts new tests pass 100% (9+ tests in new describe block)
doctor.ts new region (rc.4 TASK-001 additions) lines coverage >= 90%
0 regression in existing 15-check behavior (existing doctor tests still pass)

risks.description: events.jsonl tail scan for last-active calculation is O(N events × M files) — could become slow on large ledgers
risks.mitigation: Build a Map<stable_id, lastActiveAt> in a single pass over events.jsonl, then look up per file in O(1); document complexity in code comment; threshold for v2.0 is fine since ledgers are <10k events typical
risks.fallback: If perf becomes issue, add an index file .fabric/.last-active-index.json maintained by doctor; defer to v2.x

risks.description: Maturity tier 'draft' might have multiple semantic interpretations (newly authored vs post-demote); orphan-demote could double-demote
risks.mitigation: Orphan-demote only emits a finding (no mutation in TASK-001); TASK-003 mutation step checks current maturity == proposed_action.from before applying; idempotent re-run is safe; double-demote impossible without crossing 14d threshold again
risks.fallback: If schema needs separate post-demote marker, add `demoted_at` frontmatter field in v2.1 (additive, non-breaking)

risks.description: Pending overdue uses frontmatter.created_at which may not exist on rc.2 entries (schema landed in rc.1 but pending entries pre-existed)
risks.mitigation: Fallback to fs.stat mtime if frontmatter.created_at absent; document precedence in code; matches rc.3 pattern of frontmatter-then-stat
risks.fallback: Treat missing created_at AND missing mtime as 'flag-as-overdue' to surface for human triage

key_functions.purpose: Factory; returns {id:'lint-orphan-demote', run: async () => Finding[]} that walks canonical entries and computes per-maturity inactivity threshold
Factory for stale-archive check; gates on maturity=draft AND additional 90d quiet beyond demote threshold
Factory for pending-overdue check; flags pending entries >14d old; report only (no mutation)
Single-pass scan of events.jsonl building stable_id → lastActiveAt epoch ms map
Pure helper returning 90/30/14 day threshold per maturity tier
Pure helper computing demotion target tier; draft → null (terminal demote → archive in TASK-002 stale-archive check)
